title: Complexity and procurement risk assessment - Characteristics of procurement complexity levels | CanadaBuys

Approval authorities for Acquisitions Program clients' programs only (other than for a Canadian Commercial Corporation)
Retroactive Treasury Board approval of contracts

structure_blocks.heading: Characteristics of procurement complexity levels
structure_blocks.text: Characteristics of procurement complexity levels

structure_blocks.heading: Characteristics of procurement complexity levels
structure_blocks.text: There are five complexity levels, each requiring a different approach and level of expertise.

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 1

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 1 procurements include at least one of the following and do not include any of the criteria of the other complexity levels:

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: a call-up against a standing offer;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: a commercially available good that is readily available in the marketplace, which may include manufacturer-supplied customizable options (includes Commercial Off-the-Shelf [COTS] or military off-the-shelf [MOTS]);

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: a common service that is readily available in the marketplace, which may include supplier customized options;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: procurement methodologies that are tried and proven using existing conventions;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: departmental standard procurement templates or legally approved templates;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 1
structure_blocks.text: contractor selection methodology that is based on price and delivery alone, or where all criteria determining the successful offeror are objective (for example, grid) and do not require subjective judgement

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 2

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 2 procurements include one or more of the following and do not include any of the criteria of complexity levels 3, 4 and 5:

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: government-specified performance requirements to commercially available goods;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: a requirement which uses existing technology/processes to achieve an innovative solution;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: services with subjective results (for example, consultations and recommendations);

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: departmental standard or sector procurement templates which have been modified, that introduce new custom clauses, or that use terms and conditions other than the standard;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: one or more evaluation criteria to determine the successful offeror and that require subjective judgment;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: industrial regional benefits (IRBs) or value propositions;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: vendor pre-qualification (includes establishment of supply arrangements);

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 2
structure_blocks.text: advance payments

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 3
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 3

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 3
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 3 procurements include one or more of the following and do not include any of the criteria of complexity levels 4 and 5:

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 3
structure_blocks.text: a requirement which uses new, or a combination of new and existing technology/processes to achieve an innovative solution, and may be developmental in nature;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 3
structure_blocks.text: a requirement or procurement that is highly unpredictable due to a high level of uncertainty;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 3
structure_blocks.text: an untested procurement strategy

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 4
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 4

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 4
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 4 procurements involve transformational requirements that are enterprise-wide (across government) and include or involve one or more of the following elements:

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 4
structure_blocks.text: a high level of uncertainty;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 4
structure_blocks.text: may involve partnerships and shared decision-making with clients and multiple stakeholders within various federal government departments;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 4
structure_blocks.text: contract administration is detailed and extensive and its success is unpredictable;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 4
structure_blocks.text: more than one tier of government (federal, provincial, municipal) or multiple clients/stakeholders with decision-making authority

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 4
structure_blocks.text: Note: Complexity Level 4 procurements do not include or involve any of the criteria of complexity level 5.

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 5
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 5

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 5
structure_blocks.text: Complexity level 5 procurements involve transformational requirements that affect public policy or culture (external to government) and include or involve one or more of the following elements:

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 5
structure_blocks.text: a high level of uncertainty;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 5
structure_blocks.text: partnerships with clients, multiple stakeholders (internal and external to the federal government) and elected officials, to reshape public policy or introduce new public policies;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 5
structure_blocks.text: shared decision-making;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 5
structure_blocks.text: contract administration that is detailed, extensive and highly unpredictable;

structure_blocks.heading: Complexity level 5
structure_blocks.text: ultimate success is determined by the public at large
